briefdescription.para: This typedef enumerates the list of training states used in the state machine during the link training process.
This function is the delay/sleep function for the XDpPsu driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the number of microseconds to delay/sleep for.

briefdescription.para: This function waits for a reply indicating that the most recent AUX request has been received by the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if a reply was sent from the RX device.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX otherwise, if a timeout has occurred.

briefdescription.para: This function submits the supplied AUX request to the RX device over the AUX channel by writing the command, the destination address, (the write buffer for write commands), and the data size to the DisplayPort TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is a pointer to an initialized XDpPsu_AuxTransaction structure containing the required information for issuing an AUX command.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the request was acknowledged.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if waiting for a reply timed out.
XST_SEND_ERROR if the request was deferred.
XST_FAILURE otherwise, if the request was NACK'ed.

briefdescription.para: This function waits until another request is no longer in progress.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the the RX device is no longer busy.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX otherwise, if a timeout has occurred.

briefdescription.para: This function submits the supplied AUX request to the RX device over the AUX channel.

detaileddescription.para: If waiting for a reply times out, or if the DisplayPort TX core indicates that the request was deferred, the request is sent again (up to a maximum specified by XDPPSU_AUX_MAX_DEFER_COUNT|XDPPSU_AUX_MAX_TIMEOUT_COUNT).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

is a pointer to an initialized XDpPsu_AuxTransaction structure containing the required information for issuing an AUX command, as well as a write buffer used for write commands, and a read buffer for read commands.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the request was acknowledged.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if resending the request exceeded the maximum for deferral and timeout.
XST_FAILURE otherwise (if the DisplayPort TX core sees a NACK reply code or if the AUX transaction failed).

briefdescription.para: This function determines what the RX device's required training delay is for link training.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

simplesect.para: The training delay specified in the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) register, XDPPSU_DPCD_TRAIN_AUX_RD_INTERVAL.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the training pattern to be used during link training for both the DisplayPort TX core and the RX device.

detaileddescription.para: Scrambler isenabled when pattern is turned off.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

selects the pattern to be used. One of the following:
XDPPSU_TRAINING_PATTERN_SET_OFF
XDPPSU_TRAINING_PATTERN_SET_TP1
XDPPSU_TRAINING_PATTERN_SET_TP2
XDPPSU_TRAINING_PATTERN_SET_TP3

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the pattern was successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) indicates that the clock recovery sequence during link training was successful - the RX device's link clock and data recovery unit has realized and maintained the frequency lock for all lanes currently in use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the number of lanes to check.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device's clock recovery PLL has achieved frequency lock for all lanes in use.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) indicates that the channel equalization sequence during link training was successful - the RX device has achieved channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment for all lanes currently in use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the number of lanes to check.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device has achieved channel equalization symbol lock, and interlane alignment for all lanes in use.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function will do a burst AUX read from the RX device over the AUX channel.

detaileddescription.para: The contents of the status registers will be stored for later use by XDpPsu_CheckClockRecovery, XDpPsu_CheckChannelEqualization, and XDpPsu_AdjVswingPreemp.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the AUX read was successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function sets new voltage swing and pre-emphasis levels using the adjustment requests obtained from the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the new levels were written successfully.
XST_FAILURE otherwise (an AUX transaction failed).

briefdescription.para: This function runs the channel equalization sequence as part of link training.

detaileddescription.para: The sequence is as follows: 0) Start signaling with the same drive settings used at the end of the clock recovery sequence. 1) Transmit training pattern 2 (or 3) over the main link with symbol scrambling disabled. 2) The channel equalization loop. If channel equalization is unsuccessful after 5 loop iterations, return. 2a) Wait for at least the period of time specified in the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) register, TRAINING_AUX_RD_INTERVAL. 2b) Check if all lanes have achieved channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment. If so, return. 2c) Check if the same voltage swing level has been used 5 consecutive times or if the maximum level has been reached. If so, return. 2d) Adjust the voltage swing, pre-emphasis, and post-cursor levels as requested by the RX device. 2e) Loop back to 2a. For a more detailed description of the channel equalization sequence, see section 3.5.1.2.2 of the DisplayPort 1.2a specification document.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

simplesect.para:
The next training state:
XDPPSU_TS_SUCCESS if training succeeded.
XDPPSU_TS_FAILURE if writing the drive settings to the RX device was unsuccessful.
XDPPSU_TS_ADJUST_LINK_RATE if, after 5 loop iterations, the channel equalization sequence did not complete successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function is reached if either the clock recovery or the channel equalization process failed during training, and a minimal data rate of 1.62 Gbps was being used.

detaileddescription.para: As a result, the number of lanes in use will be reduced, and training will be re-attempted (starting with clock recovery) at this lower lane count.

simplesect.para: Training will be re-attempted with the maximum data rate being used with the reduced lane count to train at the main link at the maximum bandwidth possible.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

simplesect.para:
The next training state:
XDPPSU_TS_FAILURE if only one lane is already in use.
XDPPSU_TS_CLOCK_RECOVERY otherwise. Re-attempt training.

briefdescription.para: This function is reached if either the clock recovery or the channel equalization process failed during training.

detaileddescription.para: As a result, the data rate will be downshifted, and training will be re-attempted (starting with clock recovery) at the reduced data rate. If the data rate is already at 1.62 Gbps, a downshift in lane count will be attempted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

simplesect.para:
The next training state:
XDPPSU_TS_ADJUST_LANE_COUNT if the minimal data rate is already in use. Re-attempt training at a reduced lane count.
XDPPSU_TS_CLOCK_RECOVERY otherwise. Re-attempt training.

briefdescription.para: This function runs the clock recovery sequence as part of link training.

detaileddescription.para: The sequence is as follows: 0) Start signaling at the minimum voltage swing, pre-emphasis, and post- cursor levels. 1) Transmit training pattern 1 over the main link with symbol scrambling disabled. 2) The clock recovery loop. If clock recovery is unsuccessful after 5 loop iterations, return. 2a) Wait for at least the period of time specified in the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) register, TRAINING_AUX_RD_INTERVAL. 2b) Check if all lanes have achieved clock recovery lock. If so, return. 2c) Check if the same voltage swing level has been used 5 consecutive times or if the maximum level has been reached. If so, return. 2d) Adjust the voltage swing, pre-emphasis, and post-cursor levels as requested by the RX device. 2e) Loop back to 2a. For a more detailed description of the clock recovery sequence, see section 3.5.1.2.1 of the DisplayPort 1.2a specification document.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

simplesect.para:
The next training state:
XDPPSU_TS_CHANNEL_EQUALIZATION if the clock recovery sequence completed successfully.
XDPPSU_TS_FAILURE if writing the drive settings to the RX device was unsuccessful.
XDPPSU_TS_ADJUST_LINK_RATE if the clock recovery sequence did not complete successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function runs the link training process.

detaileddescription.para: It is implemented as a state machine, with each state returning the next state. First, the clock recovery sequence will be run; if successful, the channel equalization sequence will run. If either the clock recovery or channel equalization sequence failed, the link rate or the number of lanes used will be reduced and training will be re-attempted. If training fails at the minimal data rate, 1.62 Gbps with a single lane, training will no longer re-attempt and fail.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the training process succeeded.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the lane alignment status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the lane alignment is complete.
XST_FAILURE if the lane alignment is not complete.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpPsu *InstancePtr, u32 CmdType, u32 Address, u32 NumBytes, u8 *Data)
memberdef.name: XDpPsu_AuxCommon

briefdescription.para: This function contains the common sequence of submitting an AUX command for AUX read, AUX write, I2C-over-AUX read, and I2C-over-AUX write transactions.

detaileddescription.para: If required, the reads and writes are split into multiple requests, each acting on a maximum of 16 bytes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the type of AUX command (one of: XDPPSU_AUX_CMD_READ, XDPPSU_AUX_CMD_WRITE, XDPPSU_AUX_CMD_I2C_READ, or XDPPSU_AUX_CMD_I2C_WRITE.
is the starting address that the AUX transaction will read/write from/to the RX device.
is the number of bytes to read/write from/to the RX device.
is a pointer to the data buffer that contains the data to be read/written from/to the RX device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the AUX transaction request was acknowledged.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the AUX request timed out.
XST_FAILURE otherwise (if the DisplayPort TX core sees a NACK reply code or if the AUX transaction failed).
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the sink is disconnected from the TX.

briefdescription.para: This function waits for the DisplayPort PHY to come out of reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the PHY failed to be ready.
XST_SUCCESS otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the clock frequency for the DisplayPort PHY corresponding to a desired data rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

determines what clock frequency will be used based on one of the following selects:
XDPPSU_PHY_CLOCK_SELECT_162GBPS = 0x01
XDPPSU_PHY_CLOCK_SELECT_270GBPS = 0x03
XDPPSU_PHY_CLOCK_SELECT_540GBPS = 0x05

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the reset for each lane is done after the clock frequency has been set.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function prepares the DisplayPort TX core for use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the DisplayPort TX core was successfully initialized.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the configuration for this DisplayPort TX instance and fills in the InstancePtr->Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is a pointer to the configuration structure that will be used to copy the settings from.
is the device base address in the virtual memory space. If the address translation is not used, then the physical address is passed.

simplesect.para: Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the RX device's capabilities from the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the DisplayPort Configuration Data was read successfully.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function determines the common capabilities between the DisplayPort TX core and the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if main link settings were successfully set.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the link needs training and runs the training sequence if training is required.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS was either already trained, or has been trained successfully.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the reciever's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) indicates the receiver has achieved and maintained clock recovery, channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment for all lanes currently in use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the number of lanes to check.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device has maintained clock recovery, channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if there is a connected RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if there is a connection.
FALSE if there is no connection.

briefdescription.para: This function issues a read request over the AUX channel that will read from the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) address space.

detaileddescription.para: The read message will be divided into multiple transactions which read a maximum of 16 bytes each.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the starting address to read from the RX device.
is the number of bytes to read from the RX device.
is a pointer to the data buffer that will be filled with read data.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the AUX read request was successfully acknowledged.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the AUX request timed out.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function issues a write request over the AUX channel that will write to the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) address space.

detaileddescription.para: The write message will be divided into multiple transactions which write a maximum of 16 bytes each.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the starting address to write to the RX device.
is the number of bytes to write to the RX device.
is a pointer to the data buffer that contains the data to be written to the RX device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the AUX write request was successfully acknowledged.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the AUX request timed out.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpPsu *InstancePtr, u8 IicAddress, u16 Offset, u16 BytesToRead, void *ReadData)
memberdef.name: XDpPsu_IicRead

briefdescription.para: This function performs an I2C read over the AUX channel.

detaileddescription.para:
The read message will be divided into multiple transactions if the requested data spans multiple segments. The segment pointer is automatically incremented and the offset is calibrated as needed. E.g. For an overall offset of:
128, an I2C read is done on segptr=0; offset=128.
256, an I2C read is done on segptr=1; offset=0.
384, an I2C read is done on segptr=1; offset=128.
512, an I2C read is done on segptr=2; offset=0.
etc.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the address on the I2C bus of the target device.
is the offset at the specified address of the targeted I2C device that the read will start from.
is the number of bytes to read.
is a pointer to a buffer that will be filled with the I2C read data.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the I2C read has successfully completed with no errors.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the AUX request timed out.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function performs an I2C write over the AUX channel.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the address on the I2C bus of the target device.
is the number of bytes to write.
is a pointer to a buffer which will be used as the data source for the write.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the I2C write has successfully completed with no errors.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the AUX request timed out.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function enables or disables 0.5% spreading of the clock for both the DisplayPort and the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
will downspread the main link signal if set to 1 and disable downspreading if set to 0.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the downspread control enable was successful.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function enables or disables the enhanced framing symbol sequence for both the DisplayPort TX core and the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
will enable enhanced frame mode if set to 1 and disable it if set to 0.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the enhanced frame mode enable was successful.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the number of lanes to be used by the main link for both the DisplayPort TX core and the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the number of lanes to be used over the main link.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the new lane count was successful.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the data rate to be used by the main link for both the DisplayPort TX core and the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

is the link rate to be used over the main link based on one of the following selects:
XDPPSU_LINK_BW_SET_162GBPS = 0x06 (for a 1.62 Gbps data rate)
XDPPSU_LINK_BW_SET_270GBPS = 0x0A (for a 2.70 Gbps data rate)
XDPPSU_LINK_BW_SET_540GBPS = 0x14 (for a 5.40 Gbps data rate)

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the new link rate was successful.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function enables or disables scrambling of symbols for both the DisplayPort and the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
will enable or disable scrambling.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the scrambling enable was successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the main link.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is a control flag to enable or disable main link

simplesect.para: Enable = 1, Enable main link Enable =0, Disable main link.

briefdescription.para: This function does a PHY reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the type of reset to assert.

briefdescription.para: Contains a minimal set of functions for the XDpPsu driver that allow access to all of the DisplayPort TX core's functionality.

detaileddescription.para: See xdppsu.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 01/27/17 Initial release.
